qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of the Alliancecan Slurm portal, and what kind of data does it display upon initial login?
qa_pairs.answer: The `portal.alliancecan.ca/slurm` website provides an interface for exploring time-series data about jobs on the national clusters. When a user first logs in, it displays CPU days on an arbitrary cluster across all project accounts the user has access to. If there is no usage on that cluster, the figure will contain the text 'No Data or usage too small to have a meaningful plot'.

qa_pairs.question: How can users view their resource usage for a specific project account on the Alliancecan Slurm portal?
qa_pairs.answer: On the Alliancecan Slurm portal, users with access to multiple Slurm accounts can select a specific project account from the 'Select user’s account' pull-down menu within the 'SLURM account' panel. If this menu is left empty, the figure will display all usage across all accessible accounts on the selected cluster. Once an account is chosen, the figure updates to show its usage, and an 'Allocation Information' panel provides project details. The histogram bars illustrate the daily metric for all users in that account, with the top seven users shown in unique colours and all others in gray.

qa_pairs.question: What additional metrics and display options are available in the legend of the Alliancecan Slurm portal's usage figure?
qa_pairs.answer: The legend on the Alliancecan Slurm portal's usage figure allows users to toggle the display of additional metrics beyond user colour affiliation. These include 'SLURM Raw Usage', 'SLURM Raw Shares', 'CCDB allocation', 'Queued jobs' (representing pending job resources with narrow gray bars), and 'Total' (daily total text on each bar). Users can single-click items to toggle their presence or double-click to toggle all other items on or off.
